rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain consistent scope and visibility rules when inserting annex blocks
Preserve existing component structure and connections when adding annex blocks
Ensure inserted annex blocks are syntactically valid AGREE constructs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Use valid AGREE scalar types: int, real, and bool
Align helper declaration types with expression types to avoid type mismatches
anti_patterns: Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit port or constant declarations
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
Ensure all referenced identifiers in AGREE expressions are in scope
